title: Crispy Japanese Ginger Stir-fry
description: A fresh, crisp solo-dinner dish featuring cardamom and other Mediterranean-friendly spices for a unique twist on classic stir-fries.

ingredients.name: 1 lb boneless chicken breast
ingredients.notes: Ensure the meat is at room temperature

ingredients.name: 2 bell peppers (any color)
ingredients.notes: Use the sweet varieties for a milder flavor

ingredients.name: 2 tbsp sesame oil
ingredients.notes: For a touch of brightness and flavor

ingredients.name: 1 tsp cardamom powder
ingredients.notes: Add a unique twist with this aromatic spice

ingredients.name: 1/4 tsp turmeric powder
ingredients.notes: To enhance the vibrant yellow color of the stir-fry

instructions: Season the chicken strips with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
In a large bowl, toss the chicken with sesame oil until evenly coated. Spread them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for about 20 minutes or until fully cooked through. Remove from oven and set aside to cool slightly.
Meanwhile, in a wok or large skillet, heat 1 tbsp of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and chopped onions; sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Add bell peppers and snap peas to the pan. Stir-fry them for another 2 minutes.
Return the cooked chicken strips to the wok or skillet, add finely chopped ginger and remaining sesame oil.
Mix in cardamom powder and turmeric powder. Cook for an additional minute until everything is well combined and heated through.
Serve hot as a solo-dinner dish with steamed rice.
